description_long: This is a single-story suburban house with a cross-gabled roof in muted green and gray tones. The front features a small covered porch with a prominent overhang, supported by columns, and a main entry door flanked by windows. The exterior walls are light-colored, likely finished in siding or stucco, and the windows are symmetrically arranged with blue shutters. The roof has multiple intersecting gables, giving the house a classic, residential appearance. The rear and sides of the house are simpler, with fewer windows and no visible decorative elements. The overall design suggests a comfortable, family-oriented dwelling typical of American neighborhoods.
description: A single-story suburban house with a cross-gabled green roof, light exterior walls, and a covered front porch with columns and blue-shuttered windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, main entry door, and symmetrically placed windows with blue shutters.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the intersecting gables, a plain wall with two windows, and the depth of the house.
description_view_2: Rear view with a simple facade, minimal windows, and the back gable section visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing a long wall with no windows and the continuation of the cross-gabled roof.
attribute_entryway_design: Covered porch with columns, overhanging gable roof, and a central entry door flanked by windows
